headline: Josh Allen Voted NFL's Top QB for 2026
writeup: Josh Allen has been crowned the NFL's top quarterback for 2026 by virtually every major outlet, with ESPN's league poll of GMs and coaches calling him 'the most singularly unstoppable player at the position' — a ranking that finally and definitively settles the long-running Allen vs. Mahomes debate. The Bills enter training camp with genuine roster intrigue, particularly on offense, where coordinator changes and questions about the receiver corps loom large after an anonymous NFL coordinator publicly criticized last season's wideouts. Keon Coleman is drawing polarizing attention, with ex-Bills receiver Stevie Johnson offering a generous stat prediction while others remain skeptical, making the second-year wideout one of the most watched players heading into camp. On the defensive side, former safety Jim Leonhard is generating buzz as a potential difference-maker for the Bills' defensive tackle unit, while rookie Deone Walker has been identified as Buffalo's top breakout candidate for the 2026 season. Off the field, a bittersweet video of the old Bills stadium demolition surfaced, and Gable Steveson's NFL dream officially ended with a roster cut as he pivots to the UFC.
